mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with combined INDPRO/HOUST growth (Pearson r < 0.1) or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS and declining RSAFS (consumer credit) reduces liquidity, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines (Pearson r > -0.15) or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 200k indicates labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS exceeds 200k (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) and declining GDP (GDPC1) indicates stagflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY and GDPC1 declines > 1% QoQ
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and GDPC1 declines.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish FEDFUNDS outlook combined with rising consumer credit (RSAFS) signals accommodative policy and strong demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS stable or declining and RSAFS rises > 3% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is stable/declining and RSAFS rises.
